name: Xu Zhu (Zhongkang)
page_title: Xu Zhu (Zhongkang) “Fervent Tiger” 許褚 (仲康) “虎癡” [Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Xu Chu probably never captured of He Yi. He Yi, defeated in battle, surrendered to Cao Cao in AD 196.
	Xu Chu did not strip down and duel with Ma Chao.

sections.novel:

	Xu Chu probably never captured of He Yi. He Yi, defeated in battle, surrendered to Cao Cao in AD 196.
	Xu Chu did not strip down and duel with Ma Chao.
sections.short-biography: One of Cao Cao’s trusted guards and a commander. Leader of the Tiger Guard.
sections.details:
Nickname 
	“Fervent Tiger” (Huchi 虎癡)


	 Traditional Chinese 
	許褚 (仲康) “虎癡”


	 Simplified Chinese 
	许褚 (仲康)


	 Pronunciation 
	Xu3 Chu3 (Zhong4kang1) 


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Xǔ Chǔ (Zhòngkāng) “Hǔchī”


	 Wade-Giles 
	Hsü Ch‘u (Chung-k‘-ang) “Hu-ch‘ih”

	 Birthplace 
	Qiao County, Pei Prefecture (Presently Bo Zhou City in An Hui Province)

	 Other Names 
	Xu Chu, Marquis Tiger, Tiger Marquis, Crazy Tiger, Mad Tiger, Tiger Lust, Tiger Lord, Tiger Fool

	 Name Notes 
	His name is also frequently written ‘Xu Chu’, which is likely erroneous as 褚 is only Romanized to Chu as a surname; the more likely Romanization is Zhu. His nickname, 虎痴 (Hu3 Chi1), has been translated as Marquis Tiger, Crazy Tiger, Mad Tiger, and other variations. He also appears once in the Brewitt-Taylor translation by the nickname Tiger Lust.

full_text:
Xu Zhu (Zhongkang) “Fervent Tiger” 許褚 (仲康) “虎癡”
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Xu Zhu (許褚)aka“Fervent Tiger” (虎癡), styledZhongkang (仲康), was born in Qiao County, Pei Prefecture (Presently Bo Zhou City in An Hui Province). One of Cao Cao’s trusted guards and a commander. Leader of the Tiger Guard. Xu Zhu was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Xu Zhu lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.
